title: Pete Thomas (drummer)

Records"), [Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_Records "Mercury Records"), [Island](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records"), [Lost Highway](/wiki/Lost_Highway_Records "Lost Highway Records"), [Verve](/wiki/Verve_Records "Verve Records"), [HearMusic](/wiki/Hear_Music "Hear Music"), [Rykodisc](/wiki/Rykodisc_Records "Rykodisc Records"), [Rhino](/wiki/Rhino_Records "Rhino Records"), [Hip-O](/wiki/Hip-O_Records "Hip-O Records")

from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Pete Thomas" drummer](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Pete+Thomas%22+drummer&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2009)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(born 9 August 1954) is an English rock drummer best known for his collaboration with singer [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello"), both as a member of his band the Attractions and with Costello as a solo artist. Besides his lengthy career as a studio musician and touring drummer, he has been a member of the band [Squeeze](/wiki/Squeeze_(band) "Squeeze (band)") during the 1990s and a member of the [supergroup](/wiki/Supergroup_(music) "Supergroup (music)") [Works Progress Administration](/wiki/Works_Progress_Administration_(musical_group) "Works Progress Administration (musical group)") during the early 2000s.

Musical artist

**Peter Michael Thomas** (born 9 August 1954) is an English rock drummer best known for his collaboration with singer [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello"), both as a member of his band the Attractions and with Costello as a solo artist. Besides his lengthy career as a studio musician and touring drummer, he has been a member of the band [Squeeze](/wiki/Squeeze_(band) "Squeeze (band)") during the 1990s and a member of the [supergroup](/wiki/Supergroup_(music) "Supergroup (music)") [Works Progress Administration](/wiki/Works_Progress_Administration_(musical_group) "Works Progress Administration (musical group)") during the early 2000s.

[Tom Waits](/wiki/Tom_Waits "Tom Waits") has referred to him as "one of the best rock drummers alive."[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pete_Thomas_(drummer)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Thomas states that his favourite album and greatest influence is *[Are You Experienced](/wiki/Are_You_Experienced "Are You Experienced")* by [The Jimi Hendrix Experience](/wiki/The_Jimi_Hendrix_Experience "The Jimi Hendrix Experience"). He first heard the album at the age of 14, and became greatly influenced by Jimi Hendrix Experience drummer [Mitch Mitchell](/wiki/Mitch_Mitchell "Mitch Mitchell"). Thomas met his drumming hero as a teenager, after waiting outside Mitchell's house for multiple days.[[2]](#cite_note-thomas-podcast-008-2)

Following early work with [Chilli Willi and the Red Hot Peppers](/wiki/Chilli_Willi_and_the_Red_Hot_Peppers "Chilli Willi and the Red Hot Peppers") and [John Stewart](/wiki/John_Stewart_(folk_musician) "John Stewart (folk musician)"), Thomas was recruited as a member of Costello's backing band [the Attractions](/wiki/The_Attractions "The Attractions") in 1977. [Elvis Costello & the Attractions](/wiki/Elvis_Costello_%26_the_Attractions "Elvis Costello & the Attractions") would spend the next decade touring the world, and [recording](/wiki/Sound_recording_and_reproduction "Sound recording and reproduction") nine albums,[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3) from *[This Year's Model](/wiki/This_Year%27s_Model "This Year's Model")* (1978) to *[Blood and Chocolate](/wiki/Blood_and_Chocolate_(album) "Blood and Chocolate (album)")* (1986).

Although Costello split with the Attractions between 1987 and 1993, he continued to work regularly with Thomas during this period. Thomas played drums on the albums *[Spike](/wiki/Spike_(Elvis_Costello_album) "Spike (Elvis Costello album)")* (1989), *[Mighty Like a Rose](/wiki/Mighty_Like_a_Rose "Mighty Like a Rose")* (1991), and *[Kojak Variety](/wiki/Kojak_Variety "Kojak Variety")* (recorded in 1990 but not released until 1995) and was a member of Costello's 1989–1991 touring band, The Rude 5.

Costello reunited with the Attractions for the albums *[Brutal Youth](/wiki/Brutal_Youth "Brutal Youth")* (1994) and *[All This Useless Beauty](/wiki/All_This_Useless_Beauty "All This Useless Beauty")* (1996).

In 2001, Costello recruited Thomas, Faragher, and fellow Attraction [Steve Nieve](/wiki/Steve_Nieve "Steve Nieve") to record the album that became *[When I Was Cruel](/wiki/When_I_Was_Cruel "When I Was Cruel")* (2002). Elvis Costello & the Imposters, as they were subsequently named, have gone on to tour extensively, and recorded the album *[The Delivery Man](/wiki/The_Delivery_Man_(album) "The Delivery Man (album)")* (2004). From 2008 until 2010, Thomas was a member of the house band for Costello's television program *[Spectacle: Elvis Costello with...](/wiki/Spectacle:_Elvis_Costello_with... "Spectacle: Elvis Costello with...")*

Thomas lives in Los Angeles, with his wife Judy. Their daughter [Tennessee](/wiki/Tennessee_Thomas "Tennessee Thomas") is drummer for the band [the Like](/wiki/The_Like "The Like"). In 2003, Thomas was inducted into the [Rock and Roll Hall of Fame](/wiki/Rock_and_Roll_Hall_of_Fame "Rock and Roll Hall of Fame") as a member of [Elvis Costello & the Attractions](/wiki/Elvis_Costello_%26_the_Attractions "Elvis Costello & the Attractions").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In summer 2015, Thomas hit the road as drummer for the summer tour of the folk-pop duo [the Weepies](/wiki/The_Weepies "The Weepies").

### Studio work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pete_Thomas_(drummer)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Studio work")]

Over the years Thomas has been an in-demand session musician. His many credits include drums for [Graham Parker](/wiki/Graham_Parker "Graham Parker")'s 1991 album *Struck by Lightning* and 1992 album *Burning Questions*.

After Costello split with the Attractions for a second time, Thomas worked for the next few years primarily as a [session](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") drummer, recording with such artists as [Suzanne Vega](/wiki/Suzanne_Vega "Suzanne Vega"),[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3) [Neil Finn](/wiki/Neil_Finn "Neil Finn"), [Richard Thompson](/wiki/Richard_Thompson_(musician) "Richard Thompson (musician)"), [Vonda Shepard](/wiki/Vonda_Shepard "Vonda Shepard"), Mikel Erentxun, [Sheryl Crow](/wiki/Sheryl_Crow "Sheryl Crow"),[[5]](#cite_note-drummagazine-5) [Fito Páez](/wiki/Fito_P%C3%A1ez "Fito Páez"), [Joaquín Sabina](/wiki/Joaqu%C3%ADn_Sabina "Joaquín Sabina"), [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos"),[[5]](#cite_note-drummagazine-5) [Wild Colonials](/wiki/Wild_Colonials "Wild Colonials"), Matt Brown of [3 lb. Thrill](/wiki/3_lb._Thrill "3 lb. Thrill"), [Wendy James](/wiki/Wendy_James "Wendy James"),[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3) [Tasmin Archer](/wiki/Tasmin_Archer "Tasmin Archer") and [John Paul Jones](/wiki/John_Paul_Jones_(musician) "John Paul Jones (musician)").[[5]](#cite_note-drummagazine-5)

In 1993, Thomas joined the band [Squeeze](/wiki/Squeeze_(band) "Squeeze (band)") for their album *[Some Fantastic Place](/wiki/Some_Fantastic_Place "Some Fantastic Place")*, replacing [Gilson Lavis](/wiki/Gilson_Lavis "Gilson Lavis") on drums, and for the tour in support of it.

In 1994, Thomas was part of a [trio](/wiki/Trio_(music) "Trio (music)") that included vocalist/[avant garde](/wiki/Avant_garde "Avant garde") opera [diva](/wiki/Diva "Diva") [Diamanda Galás](/wiki/Diamanda_Gal%C3%A1s "Diamanda Galás") and former [Led Zeppelin](/wiki/Led_Zeppelin "Led Zeppelin") bassist/multi-instrumentalist [John Paul Jones](/wiki/John_Paul_Jones_(musician) "John Paul Jones (musician)"), who recorded the album *[The Sporting Life](/wiki/The_Sporting_Life_(album) "The Sporting Life (album)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3)

He also played on the [Elliott Smith](/wiki/Elliott_Smith "Elliott Smith") album *[Figure 8](/wiki/Figure_8_(album) "Figure 8 (album)")* ([DreamWorks](/wiki/DreamWorks_SKG "DreamWorks SKG"), 2000), on the songs "[Junk Bond Trader](/wiki/Junk_Bond_Trader "Junk Bond Trader")", "[Wouldn't Mama Be Proud?](/wiki/Wouldn%27t_Mama_Be_Proud%3F "Wouldn't Mama Be Proud?")" and "[Can't Make a Sound](/wiki/Can%27t_Make_a_Sound "Can't Make a Sound")".[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3)

Thomas currently plays local Los Angeles [gigs](/wiki/Concert "Concert") with bassist [Davey Faragher](/wiki/Davey_Faragher "Davey Faragher") and guitarist [Val McCallum](/wiki/Val_McCallum "Val McCallum"), under the band name Jackshit.

In 2006, Thomas played drums on the album *Doomed to Make Choices* by [Jason Karaban](/wiki/Jason_Karaban "Jason Karaban"). He also plays drums on Karaban's album *Sobriety Kills* (2009) and Karaban's single "Succeed 101" (2009).

In 2008, he joined a new eight-piece [supergroup](/wiki/Supergroup_(music) "Supergroup (music)"), [Works Progress Administration](/wiki/Works_Progress_Administration_(musical_group) "Works Progress Administration (musical group)"), with [Sean Watkins](/wiki/Sean_Watkins "Sean Watkins") (guitar), his sister [Sara Watkins](/wiki/Sara_Watkins "Sara Watkins") (fiddle), [Glen Phillips](/wiki/Glen_Phillips_(singer) "Glen Phillips (singer)") (guitar, vocals), [Benmont Tench](/wiki/Benmont_Tench "Benmont Tench") (piano), Luke Bulla (fiddle), [Greg Leisz](/wiki/Greg_Leisz "Greg Leisz") (various), and [Davey Faragher](/wiki/Davey_Faragher "Davey Faragher") (bass). The group released their debut album on 28 August 2009. Thomas played on [Willy DeVille](/wiki/Willy_DeVille "Willy DeVille")'s *[Pistola](/wiki/Pistola "Pistola")* album in 2008.

In June 2008, Thomas recorded for a [Kina Grannis](/wiki/Kina_Grannis "Kina Grannis") album. In 2009 Thomas recorded for a [Fito & Fitipaldis](/wiki/Fito_%26_Fitipaldis "Fito & Fitipaldis") album titled *Antes de que Cuente Diez*.

In 2012, Thomas was the drummer on two national tours by artist Tammy Lang:[[6]](#cite_note-6) the "Chelsea Madchen" tour, in which the singer parodied Nico, of Velvet Underground fame, and her outing as subversive traditional country-and-western alter ego Tammy Faye Starlite. Violinist [Lisa Germano](/wiki/Lisa_Germano "Lisa Germano") also played on both tours, as did bandleader/guitarist Peter "Petey" Andrews. The band "played some of the best blues rock music I've heard in years," wrote Huffington Post music correspondent Wendy Block.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2013, Thomas appeared on [Arctic Monkeys](/wiki/Arctic_Monkeys "Arctic Monkeys")' album, *[AM](/wiki/AM_(Arctic_Monkeys_album) "AM (Arctic Monkeys album)"),* on the song "Mad Sounds".[[3]](#cite_note-musicradar-3) The band described him as "saving the day" when their usual drummer Matt Helders broke his hand after punching a wall while drunk during recording sessions for the album.

In October 2013, he appeared on *[Later... with Jools Holland](/wiki/Later..._with_Jools_Holland "Later... with Jools Holland")* as drummer for [Jake Bugg](/wiki/Jake_Bugg "Jake Bugg").

In 2014, Thomas played drums and percussion on most tracks of *[Down Where the Spirit Meets the Bone](/wiki/Down_Where_the_Spirit_Meets_the_Bone "Down Where the Spirit Meets the Bone")* for [Lucinda Williams](/wiki/Lucinda_Williams "Lucinda Williams").

In 2015, Thomas played drums and percussion on award-winning Canadian singer-songwriter [Leeroy Stagger](/wiki/Leeroy_Stagger "Leeroy Stagger")'s critically acclaimed album *Love Versus*.

In 2019 Thomas co-founded the Los Angeles-based Old Man Dinner Band with [Harvey Shield](/wiki/Harvey_Shield "Harvey Shield").

## Collaborations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pete_Thomas_(drummer)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Collaborations")]

**With [Tasmin Archer](/wiki/Tasmin_Archer "Tasmin Archer")**

* *[Bloom](/wiki/Bloom_(Tasmin_Archer_album) "Bloom (Tasmin Archer album)")* (EMI, 1996)

**With [Tracy Bonham](/wiki/Tracy_Bonham "Tracy Bonham")**

* *[Down Here](/wiki/Down_Here "Down Here")* (Island Records, 2000)

**With [Jackson Browne](/wiki/Jackson_Browne "Jackson Browne")**

* *[Standing in the Breach](/wiki/Standing_in_the_Breach "Standing in the Breach")* (Inside Recordings, 2014)
* *[Downhill from Everywhere](/wiki/Downhill_from_Everywhere "Downhill from Everywhere")* (Inside Recordings, 2021)

**With [Jonatha Brooke](/wiki/Jonatha_Brooke "Jonatha Brooke")**
